title: add journald logging option
body:
Feature Request
Current state of the log driver options:

--log-driver="json-file"

Logging driver for the container. Currently not supported. This flag is a NOOP provided soley for scripting compatibility.

I'm not sure if you would rather have an overarching issue, like "add more log-driver options", or the more specific "add a journald log-driver to send container output to the host journal". I went with the latter, but I'm flexible, lmk.

comments.body:
I think the later  "add a journald log-driver to send container output to the host journal". 
If you feel adventurous to see if you could pull the journal code out of docker and see if you could open a PR for Podman.  That would really help move this forward.

@rhatdan I'd think this would need to be a Conmon change, though? That's where the container logs are handled.

So we'd need it in C, not Go.

Ok, makes sense.

I believe @nalind Did the original work on journal for Docker.  WDYT?
I'd certainly be willing to give a conmon update a shot if everyone else is too busy

I think we'll have an easier time writing to and reading from the journal in C than we had in Go.

IIRC the OpenShift teams ran up against the rate limiting that journald is configured to use by default, and they had to disable it.  The journal is doing more work for each bit of data that it gets sent, so there are situations where, without rate limiting, write throughput could be a problem.

I'm working on a solution in C. It isn't too bad because there's already a C api for journald. Just hammering out some branching logic between json-file and journald now. will open PR in conmon when it's ready.

Hello there,

While you're talking about logging - the "json-file" log-driver doesn't output actual JSON.
It looks like a simple copy of the standard ```/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay-containers/CONTAINER_ID/userdata/ctr.log```...

Care to have a look at it as well?

Cheers,

C.

@cjeanner haha yes I have found that as well. For the time being I have opted to work on changing the language to 'kubernetes-log-file' rather than 'json-file'. If there is a reasonable use-case and demand for a json file output I will look into it, but currently all that is being logged is the container stdout, for which I think kubernetes-log-file is appropriate
@haircommander Is working on this for CRI-O, once it is merged there we need to add it to podman.
@haircommander What is the latest on this?
It hit the backlog but I'll set it up next week

Also interested in this since there does not seem to be any rotation on `ctr.log` it has now filled up the disk on some of our machines with long running containers.

EDIT: It seems we can work around our issue by using this option: `--log-opt path=/dev/null` it will disable the use of `podman logs` but since our service is managed by systemd the logs still end up in journald which correctly rotate logs.
@haircommander how goes this one. any updates?
